Constructors

MemoryPool(ISystemStatusProvider, ILanguageCard, ISystemRamSelector, int, bool) 

public MemoryPool(ISystemStatusProvider status, ILanguageCard langCard, ISystemRamSelector systemRam, int poolSize = 163584, bool randomInit = false)

Parameters

status ISystemStatusProvider
langCard ILanguageCard
systemRam ISystemRamSelector
poolSize int

Properties

this[ushort]

Gets or sets a byte at the specified address (indexer syntax).

public byte this[ushort address] { get; set; }

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit address ($0000-$FFFF).

Property Value

byte

Byte value at the mapped physical location.

Remarks

Provides array-like syntax for memory access: memory[0x1000] = 0x42; Delegates to ReadMapped(ushort) and WriteMapped(ushort, byte).

Size

Gets the size of the addressable memory space (always 64KB for 6502).

public int Size { get; }

Property Value

int

65,536 bytes ($0000-$FFFF).

Remarks

The 6502 has a 16-bit address bus, providing 64KB of address space. The actual physical memory may be larger (128KB main+aux + ROM), but it's accessed through this 64KB window via soft switch-controlled bank switching.

Methods

Dispose()

Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources.

public void Dispose()

InstallApple2ROM(byte[]) 

public void InstallApple2ROM(byte[] rom)

Parameters

rom byte[]

Read(ushort)

Reads a byte from the specified address in the mapped memory space.

public byte Read(ushort address)

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit address to read from ($0000-$FFFF).

Returns

byte

Byte value at the mapped physical location for this address.

Remarks

The physical memory accessed depends on current soft switch states (RAMRD, ALTZP, etc.). This method delegates to ReadMapped(ushort) which performs the region-based lookup.

ReadMapped(ushort) 

public byte ReadMapped(ushort address)

Parameters

address ushort

Returns

byte

ReadPool(int) 

public byte ReadPool(int address)

Parameters

address int

Returns

byte

ReadRawAux(int)

Reads directly from the auxiliary memory bank, bypassing soft switch mapping.

public byte ReadRawAux(int address)

Parameters

address int

Physical address in the pool (0-65535 for aux bank).

Returns

byte

Byte value at the physical location.

Remarks

Direct Access: Reads from the auxiliary 64KB bank (offset 0x10000 in the pool). Used for 80-column display, double hi-res graphics, and debugging.

Example: 80-column text mode interleaves main and auxiliary memory for each character position, so the renderer uses this method to access aux memory directly.

ReadRawMain(int)

Reads directly from the main memory bank, bypassing soft switch mapping.

public byte ReadRawMain(int address)

Parameters

address int

Physical address in the pool (0-65535 for main bank).

Returns

byte

Byte value at the physical location.

Remarks

Direct Access: This method bypasses the soft switch mapping system and reads directly from the physical main memory bank. Used by debuggers, memory viewers, and video renderers that need to see actual RAM contents regardless of current bank switching.

Example: The video renderer needs to read from hi-res page 2 even if PAGE2 is off, so it uses this method instead of the normal Read(ushort) method.

ResetRanges() 

public void ResetRanges()

UpdateMemoryMappings() 

public void UpdateMemoryMappings()

Write(ushort, byte)

Writes a byte to the specified address in the mapped memory space.

public void Write(ushort address, byte value)

Parameters

address ushort

16-bit address to write to ($0000-$FFFF).

value byte

Byte value to write.

Remarks

The physical memory accessed depends on current soft switch states (RAMWRT, ALTZP, etc.). Write-protected regions (ROM, unmapped I/O) are silently ignored. This method delegates to WriteMapped(ushort, byte).

WriteMapped(ushort, byte) 

public void WriteMapped(ushort address, byte value)

Parameters

address ushort
value byte

WritePool(int, byte) 

public void WritePool(int address, byte value)

Parameters

address int
value byte

Events

MemoryRead

Event raised when memory is read from.

public event EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs>? MemoryRead

Event Type

EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs>

Remarks

Currently not implemented (no reads trigger this event). Reserved for future use by debuggers or profilers that need to track memory access patterns.

MemoryWritten

Event raised when memory is written to.

public event EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs>? MemoryWritten

Event Type

EventHandler<MemoryAccessEventArgs>

Remarks

Consumers (debuggers, trace logs, memory viewers) can subscribe to this event to monitor memory writes. The event includes the address and value written. Only fires for successful writes (not write-protected regions).
